perturbation.original_text: 14. These restrictions are consistent with and do not supersede, conflict with, or otherwise alter the employee obligations, rights, or liabilities created by Executive Order No. 12958, as amended; Section 7211 of Title 5, UnitedStates Code (governing disclosures to Congress); Section 1034 of Title 10, United States Code, as amended by theMilitary Whistleblower Protection Act (governing disclosure to Congress by members of the military); Section2302(b)(8) of Title 5, United States Code, as amended by the Whistleblower Protection Act (governing disclosures ofillegality, waste, fraud, abuse or public health or safety threats); the Intelligence Identities Protection Act of 1982 (50USC 421 et seq.) (governing disclosures that could expose confidential Government agents); and the statutes whichprotect against disclosure that may compromise the national security, including Sections 641, 793, 794, 798, and 952of Title 18, United States Code, and Section 4(b) of the Subversive Activities Act of 1950 (50 USC 783(b)). Thedefinitions, requirements, obligations, rights, sanctions, and liabilities created by said Executive Order and listedstatutes are incorporated into this agreement and are controlling.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. This Agreement is made and intended for the benefit of the United States Government and may be enforced by the United States Government or the Authorized Entity. By granting me conditional access to information in this context, theUnited States Government and, with respect to PCII, the Authorized Entity, may seek any remedy available to it to enforcethis Agreement including, but not limited to, application for a court order prohibiting disclosure of information in breach ofthis Agreement. I understand that if I violate the terms and conditions of this Agreement, I could be subjected toadministrative, disciplinary, civil, or criminal action, as appropriate, under the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the category of information involved and neither the United States Government nor the Authorized Entity have waived anystatutory or common law evidentiary privileges or protections that they may assert in any administrative or court proceedingto protect any sensitive information to which I have been given conditional access under the terms of this Agreement. 14. These restrictions are consistent with and do not supersede, conflict with, or otherwise alter the employee obligations, rights, or liabilities created by Executive Order No. 12958, as amended; Section 7211 of Title 5, UnitedStates Code (governing disclosures to Congress); Section 1034 of Title 10, United States Code, as amended by theMilitary Whistleblower Protection Act (governing disclosure to Congress by members of the military); Section2302(b)(8) of Title 5, United States Code, as amended by the Whistleblower Protection Act (governing disclosures ofillegality, waste, fraud, abuse or public health or safety threats); the Intelligence Identities Protection Act of 1982 (50USC 421 et seq.) (governing disclosures that could expose confidential Government agents); and the statutes whichprotect against disclosure that may compromise the national security, including Sections 641, 793, 794, 798, and 952of Title 18, United States Code, and Section 4(b) of the Subversive Activities Act of 1950 (50 USC 783(b)). Thedefinitions, requirements, obligations, rights, sanctions, and liabilities created by said Executive Order and listedstatutes are incorporated into this agreement and are controlling.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the whistleblower protection clause to a section describing the agreement's benefit to the government reduces its prominence and clarity, making it less likely to be noticed by the signatory. This affects enforceability by obscuring the protections afforded to whistleblowers.
perturbation.law_explanation: This section of the U.S. Code protects federal employees from retaliation for whistleblowing. Moving this disclosure to a less relevant section undermines the clear communication of these rights, potentially discouraging individuals from reporting wrongdoing for fear of reprisal, because they might not be aware of the protection available to them.

Prohibited personnel practices (a)(1) For the purpose of this title, "prohibited personnel practice" means any action described in subsection (b). (2) For the purpose of this section- (A) "personnel action" means- (i) an appointment; (ii) a promotion; (iii) an action under chapter 75 of this title or ot
perturbation.justification: The changed text moves the whistleblower protection clause, potentially obscuring its importance. The Law Explanation suggests this undermines clear communication of whistleblower rights, potentially discouraging reporting due to fear of reprisal. While the original text includes the whistleblower protection clause, moving it and burying it within the agreement constitutes a structural flaw that affects its clarity and enforceability. Scraped Law Snippet 2, referencing 5 USC 2302 regarding prohibited personnel practices, highlights the importance of whistleblower protections. By moving this to a less relevant section it is less likely to be noticed and thus undermines the protection afforded. This falls under the Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction definition as a required legal disclosure being placed in a misleading section.

perturbation.original_text: 2. By being granted conditional access to the information indicated above, the United States Government has placed special confidence and trust in me and I am obligated to protect this information from unauthorized disclosure, in accordance with theterms of this Agreement and the laws, regulations, and directives applicable to the specifi c categories of information to whic h I am granted access.
perturbation.changed_text: 17. I have read this Agreement carefully and my questions, if any, have been answered. I acknowledge that the briefing officer has made available to me any laws, regulations, or directives referenced in this document so that Imay read them at this time, if I so choose. 2. By being granted conditional access to the information indicated above, the United States Government has placed special confidence and trust in me and I am obligated to protect this information from unauthorized disclosure, in accordance with theterms of this Agreement and the laws, regulations, and directives applicable to the specifi c categories of information to whic h I am granted access.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the clause about government trust and obligation to protect information to the section where the agreement is being acknowledged alters the focus and emphasizes the signer's responsibility. This affects enforceability because it subtly de-emphasizes the government's reciprocal responsibility for ensuring proper handling procedures, potentially weakening claims related to government negligence in data breaches.
perturbation.law_explanation: This part of the Privacy Act outlines the government's responsibility to establish appropriate administrative, technical, and physical safeguards to ensure the security and confidentiality of records. Moving the clause that states the individual's responsibility to protect the information to the end de-emphasizes the agency's responsibility to maintain and securely protect data and could weaken claims related to government negligence in data breaches.

Records maintained on individuals (a) Definitions .-For purposes of this section- (1) the term "agency" means agency as defined in section 552(e) 1 of this title; (2) the term "individual" means a citizen of the United States or an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence; (3) the term "maintain" includes maintain, collect, use, or disseminate; (4) the term "record" means any item, collection, or grouping of information about an individual that is maintained by an agency, including, but not limited to, his education, financial transactions, medical history, and criminal or employment history and that contains his name, or the identifying number, symbol, or other identifying particular assigned to the individual, such as a finger or voice print or a photograph; (5) the term "system of records" means a group of any records under the control of any agency from which information is retrieved by the name of the individual or by some identifying number, symbol, or other identifying particular assigned to the individual; (6) the term "statistical record" means a record in a system of records maintained for statistical research or reporting purposes only and not used in whole or in part in making any determination about an identifiable individual, except as provided by section 8 of title 13 ; (7) the term "routine use" means, with respect to the disclosure of a record, the use of such record for a purpose which is compatible with the purpose for which it was collected; (8) the term "matching program"- (A) means any computerized comparison of- (i) two or more automated systems of records or a system of records with non-Federal records for the purpose of- (I) establishing or verifying the eligibility of, or continuing compliance with statutory and regulatory requirements by, applicants for, recipients or beneficiaries of, participants in, or providers of services with respect to, cash or in-kind assistance or payments under Federal benefit programs, or (II) recouping payments or delinquent debts under such Federal benefit programs, or (ii) two or more automated Federal personnel or payroll systems of records
perturbation.justification: The modification creates a structural flaw by shifting the emphasis from the government's responsibility to protect information to the individual's acknowledgment of that responsibility. The Law Explanation points out that this de-emphasizes the agency's responsibility under the Privacy Act to securely protect data. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 does not explicitly state the Privacy Act, it references 5 USC 552a, which deals with records maintained on individuals and implies government agency responsibilities for maintaining those records. By moving the clause, the contract weakens the implication of the government's legal duty, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. I hereby agree that I shall promptly report to the appropriate official, in accordance with the guidance issued for the applicable category of information, any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other securityviolation, I have knowledge of and whether or not I am personally involved. I also understand that my anonymity will bekept to the extent possible when reporting security violations.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Each provision of this Agreement is severable. If a court should find any provision of this Agreement to be unenforceable, all other provisions shall remain in full force and effect. 7. I hereby agree that I shall promptly report to the appropriate official, in accordance with the guidance issued for the applicable category of information, any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other securityviolation, I have knowledge of and whether or not I am personally involved. I also understand that my anonymity will bekept to the extent possible when reporting security violations.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the reporting requirement clause to a section discussing severability makes it less visible and less emphasized. This affects enforceability because it could discourage timely reporting of security breaches by making the procedure and obligation seem less important and prominent within the overall agreement.
perturbation.law_explanation: This section requires agencies to have procedures for detecting, reporting, and responding to security incidents. By moving the reporting requirement into a section describing severability, its prominence is reduced, potentially leading to delays or failures in reporting security incidents, which undermines the agency's ability to meet the requirements of FISMA.

Federal agency responsibilities (a) In General .-The head of each agency shall- (1) be responsible for- (A) providing information security protections commensurate with the risk and magnitude of the harm resulting from unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction of- (i) information collected or maintained by or
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the law. The original text places the reporting requirement in a prominent section (section 7). The changed text buries the reporting requirement within a section about severability (section 12), and also keeps it in section 7, potentially causing confusion and undermining the importance of the reporting requirement. According to the law explanation, this reduction in prominence could lead to delays or failures in reporting security incidents, undermining the agency's ability to meet the requirements of FISMA. Scraped Law Snippet 1, 44 USC 3554, states that the head of each agency shall be responsible for providing information security protections. By reducing the prominence of the reporting requirement, the agency's ability to provide information security protections is diminished. This corresponds to the definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction,' where a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section.

perturbation.original_text: 6. I hereby agree that I will not alter or remove markings, which indicate a category of information or require specific handling instructions, from any material I may come in contact with, in the case of SSI or SBU, unless such alteration orremoval is consistent with the requirements set forth in the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the specificcategory of information or, in the case of PCII, unless such alteration or removal is authorized by the PCII ProgramManager or the PCII Program Manager’s designee. I agree that if I use information from a sensitive document or othermedium, I will carry forward any markings or other required restrictions to derivative products, and will protect them inthe same matter as the original.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. My execution of this Agreement shall not nullify or affect in any manner any other secrecy or non-disclosure Agreement which I have executed or may execute with the United States Government or any of its departments oragencies. 6. I hereby agree that I will not alter or remove markings, which indicate a category of information or require specific handling instructions, from any material I may come in contact with, in the case of SSI or SBU, unless such alteration orremoval is consistent with the requirements set forth in the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the specificcategory of information or, in the case of PCII, unless such alteration or removal is authorized by the PCII ProgramManager or the PCII Program Manager’s designee. I agree that if I use information from a sensitive document or othermedium, I will carry forward any markings or other required restrictions to derivative products, and will protect them inthe same matter as the original.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the clause prohibiting alteration of markings to a section about other non-disclosure agreements reduces its immediate impact and visibility. This affects enforceability as it could lead to unintentional alteration of markings due to lack of emphasis, making it harder to prove intent in case of a violation.
perturbation.law_explanation: This regulation mandates proper marking of classified information. By placing the prohibition of altering markings within a section that discusses other agreements, the emphasis on adhering to classification standards is weakened. The placement can indirectly lead to the improper modification or removal of protective markings. It fails to emphasize the seriousness of compliance with marking regulations.

perturbation.justification: The modification shifts the clause regarding the alteration of markings to a different section, which discusses other agreements. This undermines the emphasis on adhering to marking standards. The Law Explanation states that this placement 'can indirectly lead to the improper modification or removal of protective markings' and 'fails to emphasize the seriousness of compliance with marking regulations.' This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction', where a 'required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section.' While Scraped Law Snippet 1 does not provide specific details on marking regulations, it does confirm the existence of such regulations and the importance of compliance. The relocation reduces the visibility and immediate impact of the clause, making it harder to enforce and potentially leading to unintentional violations.
